question_title: Will the interest in “gisele pelicot” change between 2026-02-17 and 2026-02-26 according to Google Trends?
summary: The term "gisele pelicot" is experiencing a massive news-driven spike (peak 100 on Feb 15, 67 on Feb 17) caused by the publication of her memoir "A Hymn to Life" on Feb 17 and a media blitz (BBC, AFP, NPR interviews) in mid-February. The term's baseline interest is 0-4. By Feb 26 (9 days after the comparison date), the spike will have decayed to near-baseline levels (estimated 0-10), producing a decrease far exceeding the ±3 threshold. Post-spike decay to baseline within 1-2 weeks is one of the most reliable patterns in Google Trends data. The only scenario preventing "Decreases" would be a major new catalytic event generating fresh interest around Feb 26 specifically.

factors.description: Massive spike currently at 67 (Feb 17) driven by memoir launch - term's baseline is 0-4. Post-spike decay to baseline within 1-2 weeks is extremely reliable.
The memoir launch (Feb 17) IS the catalytic event. No known follow-up events (book tour, major interview) scheduled for the final days before Feb 26.
12-month history confirms this term does not sustain elevated interest: only one prior mini-spike (Oct 2025 at 11, returned to 3 in one week).
Small possibility of secondary spike from memoir-related media appearances (US talk shows, podcast features) or new legal developments that could coincide with Feb 26.

Change_stats base rate from 3-month window: 87% no-change, 8.7% increase, 4.3% decrease - but this is misleading since most of the period was at baseline. Current mid-spike context makes decrease far more likely than base rates suggest.

**Key reasoning:**

The term "gisele pelicot" is in the middle of a massive news-driven spike (peak at 100 on Feb 15, currently 67 on Feb 17) driven by:
- Her memoir "A Hymn to Life" publishing Feb 17
- BBC Newsnight interview (Feb 12), AFP interview (Feb 15), NPR coverage (Feb 15)

The term's baseline interest in the US is 0-4. By Feb 26 (9 days after the comparison date), the spike will have decayed to near-baseline (estimated 0-10), producing a decrease of ~50-67 points — far exceeding the ±3 threshold.

Post-spike decay is one of the most reliable patterns in Google Trends. The 12-month history confirms this term doesn't sustain elevated interest (only one prior mini-spike in Oct 2025 at 11, returning to 3 within a week).

The small residual probability for "Increases" or "Doesn't change" accounts for an unexpected secondary catalyst (major US media appearance, legal developments) coinciding with the end of the resolution window.
